text: Handling deadlines effectively is a key skill in both academic and professional life. Good time management helps you break large projects into smaller tasks, making them easier to complete on schedule. Prioritization is equally important: focus on high-impact tasks first, and learn to say no to less urgent requests. Scope management means clearly defining what a project includes and avoiding unnecessary additions, which can delay progress. Finally, communication with team members or supervisors about progress and potential challenges allows for adjustments before problems become critical. By balancing these elements, you can meet deadlines with less stress.
